sections.category: LLM Workflow > Corpus2Skill / "Skills as a navigable artifact"

bullets.body: **Corpus2Skill (arxiv 2604.14572, Apr 27) — distill-corpus-into-skill-tree RAG that beats dense retrieval / RAPTOR / agentic RAG on WixQA, no vector DB at serve time** — Compiles a document corpus offline into a hierarchical skill directory: iterative document clustering + LLM-written summaries at each level + tree of navigable skill files. At serve time the agent receives a bird's-eye view, drills into topic branches via progressively finer summaries, and retrieves full documents by ID. **Highest quality on every WixQA metric** vs flat retrieval / RAPTOR / multi-turn agentic RAG. Skill directory is human-inspectable so the agent can reason about where to look, backtrack, and combine evidence across branches.

citations.label: arXiv - Don't Retrieve, Navigate: Distilling Enterprise Knowledge into Navigable Agent Skills for QA and RAG (Corpus2Skill)

bullets.body: **Externalization in LLM Agents (arxiv 2604.08224, Apr 24) — survey framing weights → prompts → skills/harness/protocol as the 2026 design pattern** — Recent history of LLM agents understood as a progressive movement outward from the model: capabilities first treated as properties of weights, then prompts/context, now infrastructure. The canonical 2026 framing for Anthropic's 17-skill repo, ClawHub, Multica, AgentCore Managed Harness, Hermes Agent.

citations.label: arXiv - Externalization in LLM Agents (2604.08224)

sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent v0.11.0 "Interface" — adoption Apr 27-29; Ink-based React TUI, native AWS Bedrock, GPT-5.5 via Codex OAuth, 5 new inference paths, 17th messaging platform (QQBot)** — Carry-forward; pluggable transport architecture under every provider. Novita Sandbox Apr 28 ships first-class Hermes-compatible runtimes.

citations.label: GitHub - NousResearch/hermes-agent

bullets.body: **Multica v0.2.18 (Apr 27) — superseding v0.2.16 (Apr 24); orchestrator-skills surface deepens** — v0.2.16 baseline: Chat V2 dedicated sidebar, right-click context menus on issues, in-app feedback flow, redesigned Autopilot modal. v0.2.18 ships in the same window as Apr 27's OpenAI/Microsoft AGI-clause unwind and the Apr 28 Novita Sandbox launch. Open-source, vendor-neutral, self-hosted; sits alongside AgentCore Managed Harness + Claude Managed Agents in the orchestration layer.

citations.label: GitHub - multica-ai/multica releases

bullets.body: **Novita Sandbox launches Apr 28 — managed NovitaClaw deployments + Sandbox Skills for OpenClaw + Hermes-compatible runtimes + persistent-node configs** — First commercial mitigation product purpose-built for the OpenClaw + Hermes + autonomous-agent threat surface. Counterpart to AgentCore microVM isolation but skill-marketplace-aware; positioned against ClawHavoc + Cisco's 36% prompt-injection finding.

citations.label: PR Newswire - Novita AI Launches Sandbox to Secure OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and Autonomous Systems

bullets.body: **Google TurboQuant (ICLR 2026, Apr 23-25 Rio; Research blog Apr 24-29 traction) — 3.5-bit-per-value KV-cache compression matching FP16 on LongBench / Needle-in-a-Haystack with no retraining; 6x compression, 8x faster attention** — Algorithm: MSE-driven Fast Walsh-Hadamard rotation + 3-bit Lloyd-Max quantization of the rotated cache. Google official Python implementation targeted Q2 2026; community impls in MLX + Triton + vLLM + SGLang already circulating.

citations.label: Google Research - TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression

bullets.body: **SGLang TurboQuant integration — PR #21419 in-tree (`--kv-cache-dtype turboquant`); follow-up #23134 fused-Triton-decode/extend kernel rewrite hits 93-105% bf16 throughput vs ~1% on the original implementation** — First-class deployment knob for compressed-cache inference; original PR had a hot-path full-pool dequant bug that crushed throughput, fix issue #23134 reads packed 4-bit KV directly during attention.

citations.label: GitHub Issue - SGLang #23134 TurboQuant fused Triton kernels

bullets.body: **PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27) — shared asymmetrically-compressed KV-pool for multi-agent LLM inference; int8 keys + TurboQuant-MSE 3-bit values via HF DynamicCache injection** — Writes a compressed cache once and injects into N independent agent contexts. Asymmetric compression preserves softmax stability (keys at int8) while squeezing values harder (3-bit FWHT-rotated Lloyd-Max). The first published multi-agent KV-cache architecture grounded on TurboQuant.

citations.label: arXiv - PolyKV: A Shared Asymmetrically-Compressed KV Cache Pool for Multi-Agent LLM Inference

bullets.body: **NVIDIA kvpress — Decoding-Press maturity; ExpectedAttentionPress with configurable compression ratios (0.7 / 0.5); AIME25 + Math500 + LongBench benchmarks; post_init_from_model hook** — Open-source LLM-KV-cache-compression-made-easy framework. Multiple compression methods + benchmarks built on Hugging Face transformers; recent releases add decoding-phase compression.

citations.label: GitHub - NVIDIA/kvpress

bullets.body: **TriAttention (MIT + NVIDIA + Zhejiang U, Apr 11) — 10.7x KV-cache compression that matches full attention at 25% higher throughput** — Carry-forward into the Apr 27-29 utility-wave window.

citations.label: Dev Journal - TriAttention: MIT and NVIDIA's 10.7x KV Cache Compression

bullets.body: **vLLM v0.20.0 path: CUDA 13.0 default + PyTorch 2.11 + Python 3.14 support; transformers v5 compatibility wave (PR #30566)** — Carry-forward; ships on torch 2.11 for CUDA. The default image (`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.20.0`) and PyPI wheel both move to CUDA 13.0.

citations.label: vLLM Releases

bullets.body: **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) — still unpatched into Apr 29; cross-server copy-paste class widening to vLLM (CVE-2025-61620) / LMDeploy (CVE-2026-33626)** — Maintainer-coordination yielded no fix more than 10 days post-disclosure; v0.5.10 ships exploitable. PoC `github.com/Stuub/SGLang-0.5.9-RCE` continues to circulate; CERT/CC VU#915947 active.

Defines a new threat-class: skill-marketplace-poisoning.
